subject: Physics
title: Protons, Neutrons, and Electrons
description: A micro-lesson that makes Protons, Neutrons, and Electrons usable.

hook.text: Every atom is built from just three particles. The combination determines what element it is.

buildup.text: Protons carry positive charge and live in the nucleus. Their number defines the element—hydrogen has one.

discovery.text: Neutrons sit alongside protons but carry no charge. They act as nuclear glue, holding protons together.

twist.text: Electrons are 1,836 times lighter than protons yet control all of chemistry through their arrangements.

climax.text: Change the proton count and you change the element. Change the neutron count and you get an isotope.

punchline.text: Three tiny particles. Every element in the universe.

quiz.question: What determines which element an atom is?
